text: Coffee and Cocoa Directory Uganda iv Coffee and Cocoa Value Chains Development in Uganda THE REPUBLIC OF UGANDA MAAIF Ministry of Agriculture Animal Industry and Fisheries Foreword Dear esteemed readers, It gives me great pleasure to present this Coffee & Cocoa Directory Uganda, the premier comprehensive guide to farmers, buyers, exporters, service providers, organizations and other stakeholders within the Coffee and Cocoa sub-sectors. The directory was developed by the Government of Uganda through the Uganda Coffee Development Authority (UCDA) with funding from the European Union under the Market Access Upgrade Programme (MARKUP). The overall objective of MARKUP is to contribute to the economic development of Uganda by increasing the value of coffee and cocoa exports with the main focus on exports to the European Union and East African Community. One of the key deliverables of MARKUP is to develop a directory of coffee and cocoa value chain actors and the related industries. UCDA is delighted to inaugurate the first Coffee & Cocoa Directory as an information tool that will facilitate the stakeholders in different ways such as: knowing who does what in the subsectors; accessing direct contacts of buyers and suppliers; increasing visibility of local produce to the regional and international markets; and facilitating business conversations and networking between actors that may result into future sales, collaborations and possible partnerships. The publication has been produced in both print and digital formats. The searchable online version can be accessed via the website of UCDA (www.ugandacoffee. go.ug). Printed directory copies are available for stakeholders at UCDA’s Kampala head office. The directory comprises business listings along the sub-sector value chain categorized into four main sections: agro-input and equipment suppliers; farmers and producers; traders, processors and exporters; and supporting businesses and iv

Most business listings consist of the basic business contact information which includes: the business name, physical location, key contact persons, telephone numbers, email addresses, website and description of products and services. Some subscribers went an extra mile to provide information on the year of business establishment, certifications, interests in the market, social media channels, membership and partnership details among others. We are grateful to the private sector businesses, farmer cooperatives, associations, organizations, government institutions and development partners who have supported this publication through sharing contact business information and cooperating with the consultant, Sharon Againe. We also appreciate guidance from Ministry of Agriculture, Animal Industry and Fisheries (MAAIF) staff that previously did great research on cocoa production in Uganda. On behalf of the Government of Uganda, we appreciate the European Union for funding MARKUP; A.E.S.A Consortium for technical assistance; UCDA staff; MAAIF team; and to all the experts for your tireless efforts and innovative ideas that made this publication possible. Our doors remain open for feedback on making this publication a better one. Thank you. text: vii How to use the Coffee and Cocoa Directory Uganda Coffee & Cocoa Directory consists of key contacts, physical addresses and brief description of core organizations and supporting businesses within coffee and cocoa sub-sectors of Uganda.  The directory bridges the information gap between actors within the Agricultural sector.  It helps you to know who does what within coffee & cocoa sub-sector s in various districts.  It also provides you with phone numbers and e-mail addresses which can facilitate you to initiate business-related conversations with different persons.  The publication enables you to access business associates, potential customers, suppliers and service providers for future sales, business networking, collaborations and partnerships.  It is a great database for contacting potential stakeholders to attend sector related events and meetings Recommendation Readers are strongly recommended to do prior investigation and follow the traditional procedures of doing business before making transactions with any party listed in this publication. Disclaimer: While reasonable precautions have been taken to ensure accuracy of contacts and information provided in this Coffee and Cocoa Directory: neither consultant, nor UCDA and their partners can accept responsibility for any damages or inconvenience that may rise therefrom. However, when an error or omission is reported, it will be investigated and edited in the future editions. vii

text: Coffee and Cocoa Directory Uganda 70 Coffee and Cocoa Value Chains Development in Uganda THE REPUBLIC OF UGANDA MAAIF Ministry of Agriculture Animal Industry and FisheriesMob: +256 782 700 010. Contact Person: Claire Mbambu, Administrator. Mob: +256 774 138 451. Description: The aim of this project is to develop a vibrant and sustainable cocoa value chain in Bundibugyo district. It is a partnership between the Government of Uganda and the European Union through MARKUP initiative focused at addressing obstacles and challenges experienced in the cocoa value chain in relation to reduced production and productivity, harvest and post-harvest losses, market access and diversification into higher export markets for smallholders. The project will enhance market access to EU and other Africa Caribbean and Pacific countries by supporting private operators in the cocoa value chains to improve quality and quantity of the produce and retain premium prices for the same through niche export markets.
